title: A worktree husk with no own .git makes git status report the PRIMARY's dirty state
description: Friction hit while building `storytree worktree prune`: orphaned worktree husks (files remain, `.git` link gone) were all classified 'dirty' and spared, because `git -C <husk> status` walked UP to the primary checkout and reported the PRIMARY's uncommitted state as the husk's own.
statement: When tooling runs `git status` inside a worktree DIR that no longer has its own `.git`, git walks up to the enclosing repo and reports the PARENT's dirty state as that dir's — so a husk-detector that trusts `git status` marks every husk 'dirty' and never reaps it.
evidence: First real dry-run: `node ... src/main.ts worktree prune` printed `DRY RUN — would reap 1 worktree(s) (1 registered, 0 orphaned); keep 556` (0 orphans, all kept). Investigation of a husk: `git -C .claude/worktrees/admiring-bartik-fe6fbb status --porcelain` returned ` M web` while that dir has NO .git file (the primary's modified `web` submodule). Fix in packages/cli/src/worktree.ts `worktreeDirty()`: guard with `hasOwnGit(dir)` (a cheap existsSync of `<dir>/.git`) before ever running `git status`, then re-check `rev-parse --show-toplevel === dir`. Same scan also took 1m55s from per-dir git spawns; batching `git branch --merged` + deferring the dirty probe to reap candidates cut it to ~13s.
impact: Cost ~2 min on the first dry-run plus the investigation to explain the 0-reap; without the guard the whole reaper is inert (nothing is ever reaped). Hits anyone building git tooling that scans worktree/husk directories on this repo (worktrees live nested under the primary at .claude/worktrees/, so the walk-up always lands on a dirty parent).
